methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals intentional community-wide AI education infrastructure that could distribute capability beyond traditional tech elites, creating peer learning networks across Richmond's workforce.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia demonstrates distributed capacity-building in an economically transitioning region, potentially creating deep AI fluency outside traditional metro hubs.

signals.title: NVTC Spotlights Region's AI Trailblazers: Meet the 2026 AI50 - PR Newswire
signals.signal_reading: NVTC's AI50 recognition program concentrates visibility and validation around NoVA's AI leaders, reinforcing the region's gravitational pull for talent and investment while other regions lack comparable recognition infrastructure.

signals.title: NoVA School Systems Grapple with AI’s Role in the Classroom - Northern Virginia Magazine
signals.signal_reading: Schools grappling with AI in classrooms suggests platform-delivered tools are reaching education broadly, but the focus on policy over pedagogy signals shallow adoption without deep technical capability development.

signals.title: AI is shaping health care. Maryland and Virginia are regulating it very differently - WAMU
signals.signal_reading: Virginia's lighter regulatory approach to AI in healthcare compared to Maryland may accelerate concentrated innovation in metro health systems while leaving rural and underserved areas without guardrails or equitable access.
